local-deployment-guide.overview: This guide defines when and how the Smart SDLC mode can run the modernized Java application locally without requiring OpenShift, Gitea, or any external infrastructure.

required-acceptance-criteria.criterion: Application must build successfully with Maven
All tests must pass with >60% coverage
Application must run locally on http://localhost:8080

deployment-trigger.rule: Local deployment is always performed after code modernization is complete.
No external services or cloud platforms are required.

guardrails.rule: Build only after all code changes are complete and saved.
Run tests before attempting to run the application.
Verify Java 21 is being used before building.
Never use Java preview features - the application must run with standard Java 21.
Ensure all dependencies are compatible with Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.x.
Check that the application starts without errors before marking deployment complete. Use IBM Bob's embedded browser feature to run deployment tests if needed.
Verify all REST endpoints are accessible after startup.

If tests fail after modernization, first check for compatibility regressions caused by record conversion, enum refactoring, stricter validation, changed null handling, changed parse behavior, changed enum descriptions, or altered helper-method semantics.
Do not proceed to package or run the application until modernization-induced test regressions are fixed.

recommended-workflow.step: Verify Java version is 21 or higher using `java -version`
Clean any previous build artifacts: `mvn clean`
If tests fail, analyze whether the failure is due to a modernization compatibility regression before making additional changes
Verify coverage meets threshold (>60% line and branch coverage)
Verify application startup in console logs
Use IBM Bob's embedded browser feature to run additional deployment tests if needed

build-verification.description: Before marking the deployment as successful, verify all of the following conditions.
build-verification.check: Maven build completes with BUILD SUCCESS
All unit tests pass (0 failures, 0 errors)
JaCoCo coverage report shows >60% line coverage
JaCoCo coverage report shows >60% branch coverage
Application JAR file exists in target/ directory
No compilation errors or warnings about deprecated APIs
No unresolved modernization compatibility regressions remain

runtime-verification.description: After starting the application with mvn spring-boot:run, verify these conditions.
runtime-verification.check: Console shows "Started Application in X seconds"
No error messages in console logs

helper-scripts.description: The lab provides helper scripts in the scripts/ directory for common tasks. These scripts include error checking and provide clear feedback.

script.name: test.sh
script.purpose: Run tests and generate coverage report

maven-configuration.description: The pom.xml must be properly configured for Java 21 and Spring Boot 3.x.

rules.rule: Never use preview features in source code
Ensure all dependencies are compatible with Java 21
Use SpringDoc OpenAPI instead of Springfox for Swagger

port-configuration.description: The application runs on port 8080 by default. Ensure no port conflicts exist.

pre-deployment-port-check.description: Before starting the application, check if port 8080 is already in use to prevent startup failures. This is especially important when running multiple Spring Boot applications or after a previous run that didn't shut down cleanly.
pre-deployment-port-check.interpretation: - If the command returns a process ID (PID), port 8080 is in use - If the command returns nothing, port 8080 is available
pre-deployment-port-check.cleanup-if-needed:
# Check what's using port 8080 lsof -ti:8080
# If a process is found, kill it (use with caution) lsof -ti:8080 | xargs kill -9
# Verify port is now free lsof -ti:8080 # Should return nothing
pre-deployment-port-check.alternative-check:
# Alternative using netstat (if lsof is not available) netstat -an | grep 8080
# Alternative using ss command ss -tuln | grep 8080

issue.symptom: Application fails to start with "Port 8080 is already in use" or "Address already in use"
issue.solution: 1. Check what's using the port: lsof -ti:8080 2. Kill the process if safe: lsof -ti:8080 | xargs kill -9 3. Verify port is free: lsof -ti:8080 (should return nothing) 4. Alternative: Change server.port in application.properties to a different port
issue.prevention: Always run the pre-deployment port check before starting the application. Add port cleanup to your deployment scripts.

endpoint-verification.description: After the application starts, verify these endpoints are accessible. Some endpoints may return HTTP redirects (302) which must be followed.

endpoint.note: Path changed from /swagger-ui.html (Spring Boot 2.x) to /swagger-ui/index.html (Spring Boot 3.x)

redirect-handling.description: Swagger UI endpoints often return HTTP 302 redirects. Always use curl -L to follow redirects.
redirect-handling.without-redirect-follow: # This will return 302 and not show the actual page curl -s http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html # Output: HTTP 302 redirect response
redirect-handling.with-redirect-follow:
# This follows redirects and returns the actual page (200 OK) curl -L -s http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html # Output: Full Swagger UI HTML page
# Check final status code after following redirects curl -L -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html # Output: 200 (if successful)

endpoint-verification.verification-script:
#!/bin/bash # Complete endpoint verification script
echo "Verifying application endpoints..."
# Health check echo -n "Health endpoint: " HEALTH_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/actuator/health) if [ "$HEALTH_STATUS" = "200" ]; then echo "✓ OK ($HEALTH_STATUS)" else echo "✗ FAILED ($HEALTH_STATUS)" fi
# API endpoint echo -n "API endpoint: " API_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/api/orders) if [ "$API_STATUS" = "200" ]; then echo "✓ OK ($API_STATUS)" else echo "✗ FAILED ($API_STATUS)" fi
# Swagger UI (with redirect following) echo -n "Swagger UI: " SWAGGER_STATUS=$(curl -L -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html) if [ "$SWAGGER_STATUS" = "200" ]; then echo "✓ OK ($SWAGGER_STATUS)" else echo "✗ FAILED ($SWAGGER_STATUS)" fi

success-criteria.description: The local deployment is considered successful when ALL of the following are true.
success-criteria.criterion: No unresolved modernization compatibility regressions remain

issue.symptom: Compilation errors or "class file version" errors
issue.solution: Verify Java 21 is active: java -version. Use SDKMAN to switch: sdk use java 21-tem
Install Maven with SDKMAN: sdk install maven

issue.symptom: Test failures, assertion failures, or NullPointerExceptions
issue.solution: First determine whether the failure is a modernization compatibility regression. Common causes include record conversion breaking legacy getters or setters, changing enum values or descriptions, changing fromString(String) behavior, adding stricter null validation, or altering boolean helper semantics such as isCompleted(). Preserve legacy behavior unless requirements explicitly require change.
Check for Spring Boot 3.x compatibility issues. Verify all dependencies are updated. Check application.properties for deprecated settings.
Use the Spring Boot 3.x path: /swagger-ui/index.html. Verify SpringDoc OpenAPI dependency is in pom.xml.

issue.symptom: curl returns redirect response instead of page content
issue.solution: Use curl -L to follow redirects: curl -L http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html. This is normal behavior for Swagger UI.
issue.explanation: Spring Boot's Swagger UI implementation uses redirects to serve the actual UI page. The initial request to /swagger-ui/index.html returns HTTP 302 with a Location header pointing to the actual UI resource. This is expected behavior, not an error.

issue.symptom: Application fails to start immediately after stopping a previous instance
issue.solution: 1. Wait 5-10 seconds for the previous process to fully terminate 2. Or manually kill the process: lsof -ti:8080 | xargs kill -9 3. Verify port is free before restarting: lsof -ti:8080
issue.prevention: Use Ctrl+C to stop the application gracefully, then wait for "Process finished" message before starting again. Add port checks to your deployment scripts.

deployment-summary-template.description: After successful local deployment, provide this summary to the user.
deployment-summary-template.template:
✅ Local Deployment Successful!
Build Results: - Maven build: SUCCESS - Tests passed: [X] tests - Test coverage: [Y]% line coverage, [Z]% branch coverage - Application JAR: target/[artifact-name].jar
Application Running: - Status: Running on http://localhost:8080 - Health Check: http://localhost:8080/actuator/health - API Endpoints: http://localhost:8080/api/orders - Swagger UI: http://localhost:8080/swagger-ui/index.html
To stop the application: Press Ctrl+C
Next Steps: 1. Test the API endpoints in your browser or with curl 2. Explore the Swagger UI for API documentation 3. Review the coverage report: target/site/jacoco/index.html 4. Make additional improvements if needed

no-external-dependencies.description: This local deployment workflow requires NO external services or infrastructure.
